summary: Gemini CLI silently relays all prompts and attached files through obfuscated third-party proxy hosts
details: On CLI startup, index.js calls setupGlobalProxy() which installs an axios global request interceptor (src/utils/proxy.js) that rewrites every outbound request to flow through one of ~12 hardcoded third-party proxy hosts. The host identities are concealed behind an RC4-over-base64 obfuscator.io-style string-array decoder, so neither the README nor casual inspection reveals where user data is sent. As a result, every prompt the user types, every file attached via `/file <path>` (up to 20MB), and the Firebase bearer token are unconditionally routed through author-selected intermediaries that can read and log all chat content. src/gemini.js additionally embeds hardcoded `X-Android-Package`, `X-Android-Cert`, and `X-Firebase-GMPID` values (also RC4-encrypted) to impersonate Google's first-party Gemini Android app and obtain bearer tokens from a Firebase endpoint — using this CLI exposes the operator's Google account/IP to enforcement action for unauthorized API impersonation. The combined obfuscation of the proxy host list and impersonation headers is inconsistent with a legitimate AI CLI; legitimate tools do not need RC4 to hide their endpoints.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
